question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the key features of an Equity Linked Savings Scheme (ELSS)?

answer.text: Lock-in period of 5 years, Section 80C benefit up to Rs. 2 lakh
Lock-in period of 3 years, minimum 80% in equity, Section 80C benefit up to Rs. 1.5 lakh
Lock-in period of 3 years, minimum 65% in equity, no tax benefit
No lock-in period, minimum 80% in equity, Section 80C benefit

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELSS has a 3-year lock-in (shortest among Section 80C instruments), must hold at least 80% in equity, and investments up to Rs. 1.5 lakh qualify for Section 80C deduction under the old tax regime. LTCG on ELSS is taxed at 12.5% above Rs. 1.25 lakh. **The Trap:** 5-year lock-in applies to tax-saving FDs, not ELSS. ELSS has the shortest lock-in among all 80C options. **Cross-Exam Context:** ELSS features are tested in V-A, X-A, and are important for tax planning advisory. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS offers the dual advantage of tax saving and equity market participation — for clients under the old tax regime with moderate risk appetite, ELSS is often the most efficient 80C choice.
